prompt:
We store subscription periods, SLA windows and retention policies as ISO-8601 duration strings (P1Y, P30D, PT4H30M...) and every service parses them with its own regex. Two of those regexes disagree about whether P1M is a month or a minute. Please build the real thing: duration.ts, a Duration type with integer fields years/months/days/hours/minutes/seconds (all six always present on returned values, negatives allowed), and these functions. Inputs may be partial objects — treat missing fields as 0.

parseDuration(text): PnYnMnDTnHnMnS with the usual rules — M is months before the T and minutes after it; PnW is accepted as exactly 7n days but refuses to combine with anything else; a leading '-' negates the whole thing; 'PT0S' is fine. We only do integers — fractional components are a hard error. And I care a lot about the error messages, this stuff gets pasted into support tickets: not-starting-with-P, no components at all ('P', 'PT'), a time designator before the T (P1H), a date designator after it (PT1D), out-of-order or duplicate components (P1M2Y, P1D2D), fractions, and stray characters (name the character) must each throw with a message a human can act on.

formatDuration(d): canonical order, zero components omitted, T only when there's a time part, all-zero renders 'PT0S', uniformly negative renders '-P...'. A mixed-sign duration ({months: 1, days: -3}) is unrepresentable — throw, message mentioning it's mixed. parseDuration(formatDuration(d)) must be the identity on normalized durations.

normalizeDuration(d): carry 60s->1m, 60m->1h, 24h->1d, 12mo->1y. Days must NEVER carry into months — a month is not a number of days, keep it symbolic. Opposite signs within the clock part or within the calendar part settle to one sign ({hours: 2, minutes: -30} is 1h30m). Don't mutate the input.

addDurations / subtractDurations: component-wise, result normalized.

compareDurations(a, b): -1 | 0 | 1 when the answer is definite, and null — explicitly — when it isn't: compare total months and total seconds separately; if the two axes disagree in direction (P1M vs P30D) nobody can say, that's a null, never a guess.

Acceptance contract: test_duration.ts, run with `node --test test_duration.ts`, do not modify it. No dependencies.
